message:
feat(core,render-svg): add pie chart IR structures and block-beta SVG styling

Core IR layer:
- Add IrPieSlice and IrPieMeta structs for representing pie chart
  diagram data (label, value, title, show_data, slices vector)
- Add pie_meta field to MermaidDiagramIr, laying the foundation for
  pie chart parsing and rendering

SVG render layer:
- Add .fm-node-block-beta CSS family styling (slate fill, dark stroke,
  light text) to visually distinguish Block Beta diagram nodes
- Add .fm-node-block-beta-space CSS rules with opacity:0 and
  pointer-events:none to hide synthetic __space_* placeholder nodes
  while preserving layout geometry
- Add is_block_beta_space_node() detection helper and wire up
  is_block_beta / is_block_beta_space flags in render_node()
- Suppress label rendering on synthetic space nodes to prevent
  "__space_0" text from appearing in output
- Add two tests: CSS emission verification and space node label suppression

Issue tracking:
- Create "Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity" epic (bd-nep0) with 22
  dependency-mapped diagram type tasks
- Create Block Beta SVG styling task (bd-nep0.1) as in-progress
- Update existing issues with test contract comments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","title":"[FNX] UX subtask: structural recommendations and remediation hints","description":"Goal
Add structural recommendation UX that translates fnx findings into concrete user guidance.

Scope
- Suggest simplifications (bridge hotspots, articulation choke points, disconnected islands).
- Provide recommendation categories with confidence and rationale.
- Support CLI + wasm payloads for front-end display.

Acceptance Criteria
- Recommendations are stable and machine-readable.
- Each recommendation includes rationale + suggested remediation.
- E2E scenarios validate recommendations for representative anti-pattern graphs.","acceptance_criteria":"Mandatory Quality and Verification Contract (applies in addition to bead-specific requirements):
1) Unit tests
- Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge cases, malformed input, and error paths for all touched modules.

2) Integration tests
- Add cross-crate integration coverage for touched parser/layout/render/CLI/WASM boundaries.

3) End-to-end tests
- Add or update reproducible E2E scripts that exercise user-visible flows affected by this bead.
- E2E scripts must run non-interactively and be CI-friendly.

4) Structured logging and evidence
- Emit machine-readable logs with at least these fields:
  `scenario_id`, `input_hash`, `fnx_mode`, `projection_mode`, `decision_mode`, `fnx_algorithm`, `parse_ms`, `analysis_ms`, `layout_ms`, `render_ms`, `diagnostic_count`, `fallback_reason`, `witness_hash`, `output_hash`, `pass_fail_reason`.
- Store artifacts/logs in deterministic, inspectable paths suitable for regression triage.

5) Determinism and regression checks
- Run repeated executions on representative scenarios (>=5 runs) and assert stable outputs/log summaries.
- Add explicit regression assertions for any metric or output changed by this bead.

6) Quality gates and build policy
- Required checks: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`, and relevant `cargo test` scopes.
- All CPU-intensive cargo operations must be executed via `rch exec -- <cargo ...>` and captured in evidence notes/logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.355492549Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["diagnostics","fnx","parser","ux","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:25:41.010443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.457686835Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:13.513701911Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:22:12.334729935Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ml2r.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ml2r.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T22:28:51.352909859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-nep0","title":"EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity","description":"# EPIC: Full Mermaid-JS Feature Parity

## Goal
Achieve 100% feature parity with mermaid-js across ALL diagram types. Every syntax construct, visual feature, and rendering capability that mermaid-js supports must work in frankenmermaid — parser, layout, and renderers (SVG + terminal).

## Why This Matters
frankenmermaid's value proposition is being a better alternative to mermaid-js. But 'better' is meaningless if users hit unsupported syntax. Every 'Partial' in the support table is a potential user abandonment. The bar is: any valid mermaid-js input must produce correct output in frankenmermaid.

## Scope
This epic covers ALL 24+ diagram types. Each has a sub-epic with granular tasks for:
1. Parser completeness (every syntax construct)
2. Layout correctness (specialized algorithms where needed)
3. SVG rendering (every visual element)
4. Terminal rendering (best-effort representation)

## Success Criteria
- Every diagram type moves from 'Partial' to 'Implemented' in the support table
- The mermaid-js compatibility test suite (bd-2xl.5) passes at 100%
- README and demo showcase reflect full support
- No valid mermaid-js input produces a parse error or blank +{"id":"bd-nep0.1","title":"Block Beta: add SVG family styling and hide placeholder space nodes","description":"# Block Beta SVG parity slice

Goal:
- Move Block Beta beyond generic node styling on the SVG surface.
- Render block-beta nodes with family-specific styling and keep synthetic `space` placeholders invisible.

Scope:
- Add explicit Block Beta SVG node classes.
- Apply family-specific fill/stroke/text styling.
- Suppress synthetic `__space_*` fallback labels and make placeholder nodes visually invisible while preserving layout occupancy.
- Add focused renderer tests.

Verification:
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fm-render-svg block_beta -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets`
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- `cargo fmt --check --package fm-render-svg`","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","renderer","svg"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nep0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nep0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-23T06:13:12.607218730Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-peow","title":"Implement click directive rendering with URL targets","description":"# Click Directive Rendering

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
click nodeA href "https://example.com" "Tooltip text"
click nodeA callback "functionName"
click nodeA href "https://example.com" _blank
\`\`\`
Nodes become clickable links. URLs open in new tab, callbacks invoke JS functions.

## Current State
- Parser recognizes click directives and extracts href/callback values
- IrNode has \`href: Option<String>\` field that stores the URL
- SVG renderer does NOT wrap nodes in <a> elements or add cursor styling
- URL sanitization IS implemented (blocks javascript:, vbscript: schemes)

## Implementation Plan
1. **SVG Renderer**: When IrNode.href is Some, wrap the node's <g> element in an SVG <a> element with xlink:href. Add cursor:pointer CSS. Add <title> for tooltip.
2. **Tests**: Verify <a> wrapping in SVG output. Verify tooltip. Verify sanitized URLs don't produce <a> elements.

## Security
- URL sanitization already in place — must verify it covers all attack vectors
- No callback support (WASM/JS only) — document as intentional limitation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:43:18.616386903Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.933197674Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T06:26:13.932777199Z","close_reason":"ALREADY IMPLEMENTED: SVG renderer at fm-render-svg/src/lib.rs lines 1671-1684 already wraps nodes in <a> elements with href, target=_blank, rel=noopener when IrNode.href is Some. Verified by code review. No work needed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["flowchart","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":141,"issue_id":"bd-peow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qj2b","title":"Implement state notes, guards, and transition actions","description":"# State Notes, Guards, and Actions

## What mermaid-js Supports
\`\`\`
stateDiagram-v2
  state Active
  note right of Active: This state is active
  note left of Active
    Multi-line
    note content
  end note
  Active --> Done : complete [isValid] / cleanup()
  Active : entry: startTimer()
  Active : exit: stopTimer()
\`\`\`
- Notes: positioned left/right of states
- Guard conditions: [condition] on transitions
- Actions: / action() on transitions
- Entry/exit actions: declared as state descriptions

## Current State
- Notes: NOT parsed
- Guards/actions on transitions: NOT parsed (treated as part of label text)
- Entry/exit actions: NOT parsed

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse \`note right of\`/\`note left of\` statements. Parse guard conditions in brackets and actions after / in transition labels. Parse \`StateName : entry: action\` / \`exit: action\`.
2. **IR**: Add notes, guard/action fields to edges, entry/exit to nodes.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Draw note boxes, show guard/action text on edges.
4. **Tests**: Notes, guarded transitions, entry/exit actions.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:45:20.380064165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.874643615Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","parser","render","state"],"comments":[{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-qj2b","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:41Z"},{"id":193,"issue_id":"bd-qj2b","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. note right of StateName: text → verify note box positioned right of state
2. note left of StateName: multi-line text → verify multi-line note
3. Transition with guard: A --> B : event [condition] → verify [condition] renders distinctly
4. Transition with action: A --> B : event / doSomething() → verify action renders
5. State with entry: A : entry: initialize() → verify entry action in state box
6. State with exit: A : exit: cleanup() → verify exit action in state box","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qsr8","title":"Kanban: Implement card metadata, WIP limits, and priority styling","description":"# Kanban Rendering Improvements

## What mermaid-js Supports (v11+)
\`\`\`
kanban
  Todo
    id1[Design] @{ priority: high, assigned: alice }
    id2[Implement]
  In Progress @{ wip: 2 }
    id3[Testing]
  Done
    id4[Deploy]
\`\`\`
- Card metadata: priority, assigned, tags via @{} syntax
- Column WIP limits: @{ wip: N } on column headers
- Ticket base URL for linking cards to issue trackers
- Card styling based on priority/status

## Current State
- Columns and cards parsed
- @{} metadata syntax NOT parsed
- WIP limits NOT parsed or rendered
- No priority-based styling

## Implementation Plan
1. **Parser**: Parse @{key: value} syntax on cards and columns. Extract priority, assigned, tags, wip.
2. **IR**: Add metadata map to kanban card nodes and WIP limit to clusters.
3. **SVG Renderer**: Style cards by priority (high=red border, medium=yellow, low=green). Show WIP limit indicator on column headers. Show assigned name as badge.
4. **Tests**: Card metadata, WIP limits, priority styling.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:12.403662639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24.600150293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["kanban","parity","parser","render"],"comments":[{"id":171,"issue_id":"bd-qsr8","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:44Z"},{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-qsr8","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Card with @{priority: high} → verify red border on card
2. Column with @{wip: 2} → verify WIP limit indicator on column header
3. Card with @{assigned: alice} → verify assignee badge on card
4. Card with multiple metadata fields → verify all render
5. Column at WIP limit (2 cards in wip:2 column) → verify warning indicator
6. Card without metadata → verify plain card renders correctly","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:24Z"}]}
